differences between two populations of Kenyan bees

page.content: The first table presents macrophyte data with headers: Macrophyte, Type, N, P, Phenolics, Cellulose, Lignin, Toughness. The first row for Ipomoea, classified as AC, has values 4.28 ± 0.91 A for N, 0.70 ± 0.17 A for P, 0.22 ± 0.01 C for Phenolics, 8.60 ± 1.39 C for Cellulose, 1.75 ± 0.90 BC for Lignin, and 58.86 ± 16.63 D for Toughness. The second row for Commelina, categorized as E, shows 3.72 ± 0.19 AB for N, 0.75 ± 0.03 A for P, 1.89 ± 1.26 B for Phenolics, 15.34 ± 1.27 B for Cellulose, 2.17 ± 0.07 B for Lignin, and 79.54 ± 11.35 C for Toughness. The third row for Nymphoides (FL) presents 3.23 ± 0.34 B for N, 0.45 ± 0.04 B for P, 2.57 ± 0.05 AB for Phenolics, 21.13 ± 1.19 AB for Cellulose, 1.13 ± 0.39 C for Lignin, and 116.41 ± 20.57 B for Toughness. The fourth row for Acorus (E) lists 2.18 ± 0.10 C for N, 0.36 ± 0.06 B for P, 3.08 ± 0.07 A for Phenolics, 22.11 ± 1.18 A for Cellulose, 1.11 ± 0.58 C for Lignin, and 176.65 ± 25.44 A for Toughness. The fifth row for Phragmites (E) shows 2.38 ± 0.30 C for N, 0.34 ± 0.05 B for P, 0.30 ± 0.01 C for Phenolics, 24.85 ± 1.09 A for Cellulose, 6.64 ± 0.57 A for Lignin, and 237.69 ± 38.57 A for Toughness. The second table contains Test Number, Common name, Brief description, Region examined, Protein, and Gene. For Test Number 1, the common name is pN/pS, with a brief description of nonsynonymous vs synonymous differences within Kenyan bees, examined in Protein-coding, with Protein Venom allergen Api m 6 and Gene GB45615. Test Number 2 has no common name, describing nonsynonymous differences between Kenyan and European Apis mellifera, also in Protein-coding, with Protein FMRFamide receptor and Gene GB51916. Test Number 3, labeled McDonald-Kreitman test, details nonsynonymous differences between Kenyan bees and Apis florea, in Protein-coding with Protein NADH dehydrogenase (ubiquinone) 1 beta subcomplex and Gene GB51330. Test Number 4 is ROH, indicating runs of homozygosity, under Genome-wide, with Protein FoxO and Gene GB48301. Test Number 5, FST, addresses differences between two populations of Kenyan bees, also Genome-wide, with Protein Neuroligin-3 and Gene GB42603. Test Number 6, Tajima’s D, discusses allele frequency spectrum, in Genome-wide, with Proteins RpA70, ZFYVE26 and Genes GB44421, GB44416.
